title: phlebodium lanceolatum var. crassinervatum
intro: Phlebodium lanceolatum var. crassinervatum, also known as the 'Blue Star Fern', is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also known to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the Blue Star Fern can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to create topical creams and ointments for skin care and wound healing.
